title: Field Note 07 — Memory and the Live Channel
description: The agent doesn't blindly follow rules. It has a judgement layer that sits between instruction and action — and that layer is shaped by signals in the live chat that no static file carries.
highlights: Read-only audit of every layer Claude reads from — global rules, per-project rules, memory, role prompts
First run flagged a commit/push contradiction loading every session
Made the case for a second design problem: live-channel discipline

title: Field Note 01 — Conversation Sync
description: Three surfaces, one backend. There is an API door — just not for me. Started with data portability, built the sync.
highlights: Two endpoints, one cookie, incremental by default
Cron every 8h; key rotation surfaces cleanly when the session expires
Surface assignment is a running decision, not a one-time call

title: Field Note 02 — Headless Parity
description: Started with a bandwidth problem. Built a template. The spec is the highest-leverage point in agentic work — depth at the foundation compounds upward.
highlights: System Understanding Gate — name existing tokens and patterns before writing code
Integration Check — verify no new values, no new patterns before commit
D2 shipped in one commit instead of three

title: Field Note 05 — Batch Approval
description: When the model shows you its limitations, the right reaction is not frustration, but anticipation. The work isn't predicting harder; it's anticipating for the agent.
highlights: Permissions accumulated 135 entries across four files in three days
log-tool-use.sh + approval-report.js — coverage report with suggested wildcards
Wired into the session debrief — every close-out checks coverage
